title: ROLE.md frontmatter (AIP-47)
description: Validates the YAML frontmatter portion of an AIP-47 ROLE.md manifest. Single-doc, no oneOf — every role is the same shape. The body is markdown and not validated by this schema.

schema.description: Schema dispatch tag. MUST be 'role/v1' for this version of AIP-47.

name.description: Machine identifier. Lowercase, digits, dashes. Must start with a letter, end with a letter or digit. Unique within the registry that hosts the role.

description.description: One-paragraph job description. The role's purpose, audience, and shape.

version.description: Semver. Bump on breaking change to responsibilities, KPIs, tools, or seniority.

extends.description: Reference to a parent ROLE. Triggers composition. Accepts any of the ref forms in AIP-47 §Reference syntax: bare slug (`seo-specialist`), scoped slug (`builtin/seo-specialist`, `file/<path>`, `db/<id>`), `ws://roles/<slug>` URI, or fully-qualified URL. Path-form refs SHOULD end in 'ROLE.md'.

department.description: Department slug. Lowercase kebab-case. Recommended values listed in AIP-47 §Departments; free-form to accept domain-specific values.

reports_to.description: Role-to-role reporting link. Conventionally a 'ws://roles/<slug>' ref. Schema does not validate scheme; the loader resolves.

seniority.description: Seniority level of the job. Operator instances inherit this; a promotion is re-binding the operator to a higher-seniority role.

mission.description: One-paragraph mission, markdown. The reason the role exists.

responsibilities.description: Ordered imperative clauses. At least one. Lineage accumulates under 'extends' (append-and-dedupe).

capabilities.description: Competences this role is expected to have. Append-and-dedupe across 'extends'.

tools.description: AIP-14 tool refs the role declares as intended. Append-and-dedupe across 'extends'. Governance (AIP-7) gates effect.

skills.description: AIP-3 skill refs the role expects to load. Append-and-dedupe across 'extends'.

kpis.description: Scorer slugs (forward AIP) — KPIs evaluated against operators in this role. Append-and-dedupe across 'extends'.

strengths.description: Job-side strengths in prose. Append-and-dedupe across 'extends'.

antiPatterns.description: What the role explicitly does NOT do. Append-and-dedupe across 'extends'.

onPromotion.description: AIP-39 action ref fired on promotion INTO this role.

onDemotion.description: AIP-39 action ref fired on demotion OUT of this role.

onAssign.description: AIP-39 action ref fired on first assignment of this role.

appliesTo.description: Cross-AIP bindings — restrict the role to specific operators. Local-only under 'extends' (NOT inherited).

defaultPersona.description: AIP-25 PERSONA ref recommended for operators adopting this role. Advisory; the operator's own 'persona' field overrides.

defaultIdentity.description: AIP-23 IDENTITY ref recommended for operators adopting this role. Advisory; the operator's own 'identity' field overrides.

defaultPolicy.description: AIP-38 POLICY ref recommended for operators adopting this role. ADVISORY ONLY — role declares intent, policy decides effect. The runtime MUST NOT apply defaultPolicy to an operator without the operator's own 'policy:' field or a governance signature attesting the binding. See AIP-47 §Role vs Policy vs Governance.

packs.description: AIP-10 knowledge-pack refs this role mounts as its generic corpus floor. The host resolves each ref to a pack and mounts it read-only UNDER the operator's own editable corpus (the host overlays them). Append-and-dedupe across 'extends'. Absent = the host's convention (e.g. a pack named after the role slug). Operator-level packs layer ABOVE these.

knowledge.description: Generic knowledge this role consults — AIP-10 corpus packs mounted read-only as a floor beneath the operator's own corpus. Distinct from 'skills' (what the role knows how to DO): knowledge is what it CONSULTS. The host unions these with any operator-level packs.

tags.description: Catalog tags. Lowercase kebab-case. Append-and-dedupe across 'extends'.

metadata.description: Vendor extensions, namespaced under '<vendor>'. Hosts MUST tolerate unknown vendor namespaces; they MUST NOT honour metadata that overrides AIP-defined fields.
